slides.notes: Full-bleed custom layout + custom gradient-hero block. None of this is possible with the built-in layouts alone — it's arbitrary React, registered as a block.

props.title: Total creative freedom
props.sub: Every slide here is structured deck.json — rendering arbitrary React you registered once.

slides.notes: A motion moment — the marquee block is a CSS keyframe loop. Built-ins can't do this; a custom block can.
Custom asymmetric split layout (aside + main). Aside mixes a built-in heading with a custom pill-row; main is a custom feature-cards grid.

items.title: Custom blocks
items.desc: Any React component, registered by name and placed from deck.json.

items.title: Custom layouts
items.desc: Define your own slot regions — like the bleed and split layouts in this deck.

items.title: Generic editor
items.desc: Tweak any custom block's props right in the WYSIWYG.

slides.notes: A custom big-stat block: gradient-clipped numeral the size of the canvas.

props.label: React, anywhere
props.caption: If you can build it in React, you can register it as a block and place it anywhere.

slides.notes: The escape hatch, shown literally: a custom code-window block displaying how a block is registered.

props.text: Register once. Use everywhere — by id, in JSON.

props.title: blocks/index.tsx

slides.notes: A sampler of what's in this very deck — all custom blocks.
Ties back to agent customization — the same custom blocks are editable by conversation.

props.text: Tell an agent what you want — it edits deck.json (built-in or custom blocks) and the slide updates live.

slides.notes: Custom quote-spotlight: oversized gradient quotation mark.

props.text: Generate with an agent. Refine by hand, by click, or by conversation.

slides.notes: Close on the freedom promise with another full-bleed hero.

props.title: Your slides. Your components. Your rules.
props.sub: The document model keeps it editable. The registry keeps it limitless.
